CLARITY.justification: The paper is overall well organized (Intro → Related Work → Design Patterns → Experiments → Conclusion) and states its main contributions early (14 design patterns and several novel architectures are introduced in Section 1 and enumerated in Section 3). Notation is informal but consistent for a primarily conceptual paper, and appendices (A and B) help clarify architecture relationships and implementation. Presentation issues are minor but notable: many figure/table references (e.g., Figure 1, Table 1) are used without the visuals in the provided text, and there are formatting/repetition artifacts and a few awkward phrasings in the tail of the document that reduce polish (duplicated paragraphs and some citation/parentheses glitches).

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper's main contribution is a synthesis/perspective: articulating 14 design patterns for CNNs is a useful, but largely integrative, contribution that consolidates known ideas (e.g., normalization, skip connections, pyramidal channels) rather than proposing wholly new theory (Sec. 3). The proposed architectural variants (Fractal of FractalNet, freeze-drop-path / Stagewise Boosting Networks, and Taylor Series Networks) are modest structural combinations or re-interpretations of prior work (builds on FractalNet Larsson et al. 2016, drop-path Huang et al. 2016b, freezing ideas in RNNs Krueger et al. 2016, and polynomial/multiplicative nets Livni et al. 2014 / Lin et al. 2015), so they are incremental extensions. Overall the paper offers a clear, practical perspective and a few small novel variations, but not highly original or transformative methods.

SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper’s main claims (a set of CNN design patterns and three architectural variants: FoF, SBN, TSN) are stated and supported by limited CIFAR-10/100 experiments that show some empirical behavior (e.g. FoF trains faster, freeze-drop-path slows final convergence) but the experimental evidence is narrow (single dataset family, single baseline FractalNet, no statistical tests or multiple seeds). Mathematical/derivational content is informal (e.g. concat vs sum as weight-sharing special case in Appendix A) and not proven rigorously, and several proposed ideas (Taylor Series squaring branches, freeze-drop-path dynamics) lack analysis of stability or broader validation. Overall the technical arguments are plausible but have notable methodological gaps and unsupported generality claims, so the work is partially but not fully substantiated.
